developers.name: Robert Leshner
developers.activity: Protocol architecture and daily GitHub commits. Active on governance forums daily. Public speaker at ETH conferences.

developers.name: Geoffrey Hayes
developers.activity: Core smart contract engineering, security audit liaison, cToken protocol development. 10+ commits per week.

developers.name: Calvin Liu
developers.activity: Strategy and institutional partnerships. Former a16z crypto, deep institutional network. Onboarding major DeFi funds and protocols.

narrativeAtSnapshot: Compound Finance is the leading decentralized lending protocol on Ethereum, reporting $520M total value locked just two weeks after launching the COMP governance token on June 15th. Founders Robert Leshner (ex-Postmates, Columbia engineering) and Geoffrey Hayes represent an elite technical pair — 12+ smart contract commits per week, two completed Trail of Bits security audits, no critical vulnerabilities found. Institutional backing: Andreessen Horowitz, Paradigm, Coinbase Ventures ($25M total raised). COMP token distributes daily to users who supply or borrow assets — creating immediate yield-farming demand. Weekly mention growth: 4.2x across DeFi Telegram groups and Ethereum developer Discord servers. Protocol revenue: $2.1M per week in interest spread, growing 18% week-over-week. However, critical structural weakness: COMP is a governance-only token with zero value capture mechanism. Token holders vote on protocol parameters but receive no share of the $2.1M weekly interest income. This contrasts sharply with models like MKR where token holders benefit directly from protocol revenue. On-chain analysis: governance forum thread titled 'What gives COMP intrinsic value?' has 340 responses — the community itself cannot answer this question. Liquidity mining creates persistent sell pressure as farmers dump immediately. Mainstream coverage now daily: Bloomberg DeFi column, Coinbase blog, CoinDesk front page. Signal arrived post-mainstream.
